title: Export
content_html:
Export

Docs Viewer export converts existing generated Docs Viewer payloads into a standalone static HTML folder for a repo-backed local scope.

The static HTML export is intentionally separate from public-scope Publish and from Analytics Data Sharing exports. It does not deploy content, copy public route assets, package JSONL data, parse Markdown, or rebuild generated docs. It reads generated Docs Viewer payloads and writes local HTML files that can be opened directly from file://.

Scope

The export is available only for repo-backed local Docs Viewer scopes, such as studio.

Supported scope requirements:

  • scope_type: local
  • include_scope_param: true
  • the published docs location uses the repository provider
  • generated payloads include index-tree.json and by-id/<doc_id>.json
  • $DOTLINEFORM_PROJECTS_BASE_DIR is set to an absolute path

Rejected scope families:

  • public read-only scopes such as library, analysis, and moments
  • local_external scopes
  • scopes whose source or published docs location is outside the repository

External local scopes need a separate portability decision because their data and filesystem ownership live outside this repository.

Output

Apply writes to:

$DOTLINEFORM_PROJECTS_BASE_DIR/docs-export/<scope>/

For studio, the output shape is:

$DOTLINEFORM_PROJECTS_BASE_DIR/docs-export/studio/
  index.html
  styles.css
  docs/
    <doc_id>.html

Every apply run wipes the destination scope folder first, then writes the current export. There is no stale-file manifest, partial sync, rollback record, or delete bookkeeping. Delete Scope does not remove static export output; the user owns cleanup of stale export folders.

Input Payloads

The exporter reads existing generated Docs Viewer payloads:

docs-viewer/scopes/<scope>/published/documents/index-tree.json
docs-viewer/scopes/<scope>/published/documents/by-id/<doc_id>.json

index-tree.json supplies document order and hierarchy. Each referenced by-id payload supplies the rendered content_html and metadata used by the document page.

The exporter does not read docs-viewer/scopes/<scope>/source/documents/*.md, parse front matter, render Markdown, or apply viewability filtering. All generated docs in the selected scope tree are exported, including docs that are not public-viewable.

Service Owner

The backend owner is:

docs-viewer/services/docs_static_html_export.py

Core responsibilities:

  • validate that the requested scope is repo-backed local
  • resolve published document input paths from docs-viewer/config/scopes/docs_scopes.json
  • resolve the destination under $DOTLINEFORM_PROJECTS_BASE_DIR/docs-export/<scope>/
  • load index-tree.json
  • collect ordered doc ids from the nested tree
  • validate doc ids as safe HTML filenames
  • load each by-id/<doc_id>.json payload
  • render root index.html
  • render one document HTML page per doc
  • render shared styles.css
  • rewrite internal Docs Viewer links
  • replace the destination folder on apply
  • delete the destination folder on delete

The service is independent from Docs Viewer frontend modules.

Management API

The management-only endpoints are:

POST /docs/export/static-html/apply
POST /docs/export/static-html/delete

Apply request shape:

{
  "scope": "studio",
  "action": "export"
}

Apply response includes:

  • schema_version: docs_static_html_export_v1
  • operation: apply
  • scope
  • document_count
  • file_count
  • default_doc_id
  • absolute destination
  • display-safe destination_label
  • summary_text

Delete request shape uses the same scope. Delete only needs a valid repo-backed local scope and destination path; it does not require generated payload files to still exist.

Route constants live in:

docs-viewer/services/docs_management_routes.py

Dispatch is owned by:

docs-viewer/services/docs_management_service.py

Capability Projection

Management capabilities expose static HTML export availability per scope.

Global capability:

{
  "static_html_export": {
    "apply": true,
    "delete": true
  }
}

Per-scope capability:

{
  "static_html_export": {
    "apply": true,
    "delete": true,
    "destination": "/docs-export/studio/",
    "document_count": 232,
    "default_doc_id": "docs-viewer",
    "error": ""
  }
}

Capability projection is owned by:

docs-viewer/services/docs_management_capabilities_service.py

The frontend uses this capability to show the Export action only when the active scope supports static HTML export.

Frontend Ownership

The management Actions menu includes an Export item in:

docs-viewer/runtime/js/management/docs-viewer-management-actions-renderer.js

The action is hidden unless scopeStaticHtmlExportSupported() returns true for the current scope. Capability helpers live in:

docs-viewer/runtime/js/management/docs-viewer-management-capabilities.js

The client call is:

applyManagedDocsStaticHtmlExport()

in:

docs-viewer/runtime/js/management/docs-viewer-management-client.js

The action controller opens a confirmation modal showing:

  • source scope
  • document count
  • default document
  • destination folder

If the user confirms, the modal closes and the action controller sends the apply request. While the export is running, manage mode uses the existing managementBusy state, which sets the waiting cursor through docs-viewer/static/css/docs-viewer-manage.css.

The export action stays separate from public-scope Publish.

HTML Rendering

The root index.html renders a nested tree from index-tree.json and links to:

docs/<doc_id>.html

Each document page renders:

<!doctype html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
  <meta charset="utf-8">
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1">
  <title>{{ escaped title }}</title>
  <link rel="stylesheet" href="../styles.css">
</head>
<body>
  <nav><a href="../index.html">Index</a></nav>
  <main>
    {{ content_html }}
  </main>
</body>
</html>

The page template does not inject an additional heading. Generated content_html already includes the source document heading.

Metadata such as <title> is HTML-escaped. content_html is inserted as generated by the existing Docs Viewer builder.

Inline Mermaid Boundary

Inline Mermaid Rendering is shipped for repo-backed local readers. Static HTML export deliberately remains source-preserving: it carries the generated language-mermaid code block unchanged and does not run the Mermaid CLI, add a browser Mermaid bundle, create an inline diagram host, or write SVG files.

The resulting static page retains readable Mermaid source rather than a rendered diagram. The separate Markdown document-content export also retains the mermaid fence language and exact inline diagram source through its generated-HTML-to-Markdown transform. Pre-rendering for a reader-oriented export may be considered when a concrete consumer needs it; no rendered-versus-inline option is added in advance.

Link Rewriting

The exporter rewrites internal Docs Viewer links for the exported scope:

/docs/?scope=<scope>&doc=<doc_id>

Document-page links become:

<doc_id>.html

Root index links are rendered as:

docs/<doc_id>.html

Fragments are preserved. External links and links to other scopes are left unchanged. The exporter does not validate whether rewritten doc links point to an exported document.

Verification

Focused coverage lives in:

docs-viewer/tests/python/test_docs_static_html_export.py
docs-viewer/tests/python/test_docs_management_routes.py

The static export tests cover:

  • tree doc-id collection
  • doc payload loading
  • safe doc-id validation
  • per-doc page rendering
  • inline Mermaid code-block retention without SVG or browser runtime injection
  • no duplicate template heading
  • index page rendering
  • internal link rewriting
  • destination path validation
  • public and local-external scope rejection
  • apply response through management dispatch
  • delete behavior without generated payload files

Recommended checks after changing this workflow:

python3 -m pytest docs-viewer/tests/python/test_docs_static_html_export.py docs-viewer/tests/python/test_docs_management_routes.py
python3 -m py_compile docs-viewer/services/docs_static_html_export.py docs-viewer/services/docs_management_routes.py docs-viewer/services/docs_management_service.py docs-viewer/services/docs_management_capabilities_service.py
node --check docs-viewer/runtime/js/management/docs-viewer-management-actions-renderer.js
node --check docs-viewer/runtime/js/management/docs-viewer-management-actions.js
node --check docs-viewer/runtime/js/management/docs-viewer-management-capabilities.js
node --check docs-viewer/runtime/js/management/docs-viewer-management-client.js
node --check docs-viewer/runtime/js/management/docs-viewer-management.js
git diff --check
